text: This PR addresses issue #107 (accessibility audit of frontend/components/) across 73 files. The changes are largely well-executed: focus-visible rings on most interactive elements, touch targets meeting WCAG 2.5.8 44px minimum, motion-safe animation prefixes, theme token migration from hardcoded colors, ARIA attribute additions (aria-pressed, aria-label, role, aria-describedby), and a useFocusTrap integration. Found 1 critical bug (stale closure in mutation callback), 7 important accessibility gaps (missing focus rings, incomplete brand token migration, missing motion-safe prefix), and several minor issues.

reasoning: One critical stale-closure bug in BadgeApprovalQueue.tsx and several important missing focus-visible rings and a missing motion-safe prefix. All issues are straightforward to fix with low regression risk.

message: Integrated useFocusTrap hook — correctly traps Tab focus within dialog, auto-focuses first element, restores focus on close. Solves two audit issues in one change.
Added scope="col" to table header cells and min-h-[44px] on Manage link — improves screen reader semantics and touch targets.
Semantic improvement: div → <nav aria-label="Pagination"> with aria-hidden on icon-only children. Makes control discoverable by screen readers.
Added role="status" + aria-label="Loading your profile" on loading spinner — assistive tech announces loading state.
Thorough theme-token migration: all hardcoded emerald/teal values replaced with brand-* tokens and dark-mode variants.
Migrated hardcoded green colors to text-brand-success / bg-brand-success — consistent theme token usage.
Migrated hardcoded red/amber error colors to text-brand-error / text-brand-accent — proper semantic tokens.
Added close button, Escape key handler, and backdrop click handler — fixes the keyboard trap critical issue.
Added aria-pressed={selected.includes(opt)} — communicates selected state to screen readers.
Prefixed animate-spin with motion-safe: — respects prefers-reduced-motion.
Replaced hardcoded red classes with bg-brand-error/10, border-brand-error/20 and added role="alert" to error banner.
Added Escape key handler for notifications dropdown dismissal — critical for keyboard users.
Replaced emoji medals (🥇🥈🥉) with Lucide Medal icons — aligns with project icon conventions.
Added subscribing state guard — prevents double-submit on rapid clicks during async subscribe().

Stale closure: reviewMutation.variables reads reactive React state instead of the mutation's onSuccess callback variables parameter. In React 19 concurrent rendering, the closure may reference stale state. The mutation's own variables should be used via onSuccess: (_data, variables) => {...} referencing variables.action directly. Not just an accessibility concern — this is a correctness bug that could cause the wrong action to be reflected post-mutation.
suggestion: Change onSuccess: () => { invalidateReview(variables.action); } to onSuccess: (_data, variables) => { invalidateReview(variables.action); } and remove the reviewMutation.variables access.

message: Dropdown menu with role="menu" lacks keyboard navigation — no arrow key handling, no Enter/Space activation. WCAG 4.1.2 requires menuitems to support keyboard interaction per ARIA authoring practices.
suggestion: Add onKeyDown handler for ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Home/End navigation and ensure menuitems respond to Enter/Space.

message: Recurring toggle switch has transition classes without motion-safe: prefix. The virtual toggle (line 242) was fixed with motion-safe: but both recurring toggles (lines 99 & 146) were missed.
suggestion: Add motion-safe: prefix to transition-colors duration-200 and transition-transform duration-200 on the recurring toggle wrapper and knob classes.

message: Frequency selection buttons (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/CUSTOM) are interactive buttons that modify form state but lack focus-visible:ring.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none to the frequency button className.

message: Day-of-week selection buttons (Sun-Sat) are interactive buttons that modify form state but lack focus-visible:ring.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none to the day-of-week button className.

message: Newly added close button (X icon) is missing focus-visible:ring. Every other interactive element in this PR received focus rings but this was overlooked.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none to the close button className.

message: Add certification button is missing focus-visible:ring. All other interactive elements in this PR received focus rings but this was overlooked.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-primary focus-visible:outline-none to the button className.

message: Error state uses hardcoded red colors (bg-red-100, text-red-500, text-red-600) instead of theme tokens like bg-brand-error/10, text-brand-error. The PR added dark:text-red-400 but didn't migrate to brand tokens, breaking the project convention.
suggestion: Replace bg-red-100 with bg-brand-error/10, text-red-500 with text-brand-error, text-red-600 with text-brand-error/80.

message: Last <th> (action column) missing scope="col" — inconsistent with other five header cells that were fixed.
suggestion: Add scope="col" to the action column <th> for consistency.

message: Dismiss and install buttons use focus-visible:ring-white/50 as ring color. Against the brand-primary/90 gradient background, white/50 yields ~2.5:1 contrast — below the 3:1 minimum for focus indicators (WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11).
suggestion: Use focus-visible:ring-white/70 or focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white for better contrast.

message: focus-visible:rounded applies default border-radius — should use focus-visible:rounded-md or focus-visible:rounded-sm to match sibling element border radii.
suggestion: Replace focus-visible:rounded with focus-visible:rounded-sm or focus-visible:rounded-md.

message: Remote toggle switch has focus-visible:ring-2 but is missing focus-visible:outline-none. The div with tabIndex={0} will show default browser outline alongside the ring shadow, creating a double focus indicator.
suggestion: Add focus-visible:outline-none to match other fixed elements (e.g., line 290).

message: role="form" is applied to a wrapper div that contains a native <form> element, creating a redundant/confusing landmark structure.
suggestion: Put role="form" on the <form> element directly or remove it since native form already has the role.

message: Uses text-destructive instead of text-brand-error — inconsistent with the token standardization pattern adopted elsewhere in this PR (e.g., LevelProgressCard.tsx).

Backdrop div has role="button" + tabIndex={-1} — the role="button" makes it discoverable via screen reader element list but tabIndex={-1} prevents keyboard focus, making the onKeyDown handler unreachable.
suggestion: Either make tabIndex={0} with focus management, or remove role="button" and onKeyDown since Escape key and close button already handle dismissal.

message: Install button has focus-visible:ring-2 but the effect may be invisible on the brand-cta background if the ring color inherits poorly. Verify the ring provides >=3:1 contrast against the button background.
suggestion: Consider focus-visible:ring-white/80 or focus-visible:ring-offset-2 for the install button on the CTA background.

message: motion-safe:animate-spin correctly respects reduced motion, but the spinner border-current/30 border-t-current on the cta variant (bg-brand-cta text-white) creates a white-on-white spinner — invisible until it spins.
suggestion: Consider a fixed contrast color pair for spinner on cta variant buttons.
